items.title: The Path, founded by Tony Robbins and Calm alums, hopes to offer safer AI therapy
items.summary: The Path says its AI model has scored 95 on the mental health safety AI benchmark, Vera-MH. This compares to a top score of 65 for the consumer bots.

items.title: Hark raises $700M Series A for its secretive “universal” AI interface
items.summary: Brett Adcock's new AI startup is now valued at $6 billion by its investors.

items.title: Maka Kids is redefining kids’ screen time with a streaming app optimized for well-being, not engagement
items.summary: Maka Kids is building a streaming app for children ages zero to six featuring content designed for healthy development. The startup has now raised $3 million in seed funding to scale its platform.

items.title: Google is pitching an AI agent ecosystem to consumers who may not buy it
items.summary: The AI agents are coming. A lot of them.

items.title: Wayve’s self-driving tech is headed to US cars made by Stellantis
items.summary: Wayve's self-driving tech will show up in Stellantis vehicles in 2028.

items.title: With aluminum prices up 20%, recycling startups bet on AI to cash in
items.summary: Recycling startups are using AI to improve the recovery of critical minerals like aluminum, aiming to build a massive source of the metal.

items.title: Scammers are abusing an internal Microsoft account to send spam links
items.summary: The loophole allows spammers and scammers to send emails from a legitimate Microsoft email address typically used for sending genuine account alerts.

items.title: Beauty booking startup Fresha hits $1 billion valuation with KKR backing
items.summary: Beauty and wellness booking marketplace Fresha says it has raised $80 million investment from KKR’s Next Generation Technology Growth fund, KKR's growth equity arm.

items.title: Truecaller gets into the eSIM business to diversify its revenue streams
items.summary: The company said its plans will range from 1 GB over 7 days to 20 GB over 30 days. Initially, the launch will make the eSIM product available in 29 countries.

items.title: General Catalyst just led a $63M bet on India’s travel payments market
items.summary: Scapia, an Indian startup that combines travel booking with co-branded credit cards and mobile payments, said the deal doubles its valuation.

items.title: Imperagen raises £5 million to use quantum physics, AI on enzyme engineering
items.summary: Biotech company Imperagen announced on Thursday a £5 million ($6.7 million) seed round led by PXN Ventures, with participation from IQ Capital and Northern Gritstone.

items.title: Jensen Huang says he’s found a ‘brand new’ $200B market for Nvidia
items.summary: The next big thing for Nvidia will be CPUs for AI agents, $200 billion worth, CEO Jensen Huang predicts.

items.title: Anthropic says it’s about to have its first profitable quarter

items.title: I review robot vacuums for a living, ask me anything!
